name: libradare2-common
title: libradare2-common
summary: arch independent files from the radare2 suite
description:
arch independent files from the radare2 suite
The project aims to create a complete, portable, multi-architecture,
unix-like toolchain for reverse engineering.
.
It is composed by an hexadecimal editor (radare) with a wrapped IO
layer supporting multiple backends for local/remote files, debugger
(OS X, BSD, Linux, W32), stream analyzer, assembler/disassembler (rasm)
for x86, ARM, PPC, m68k, Java, MSIL, SPARC, code analysis modules and
scripting facilities. A bindiffer named radiff, base converter (rax),
shellcode development helper (rasc), a binary information extractor
supporting PE, mach0, ELF, class, etc. named rabin, and a block-based
hash utility called rahash.
.
This package provides the arch independent files from radare2.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use libradare2-common because arch independent files from the radare2 suite.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use libradare2-common as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair libradare2-common with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `libradare2-common --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
